info.title: sbol-db
info.description:
A Postgres-backed data management system for synthetic biology data. It ingests SBOL documents in any spec-listed RDF serialization, projects them into a typed relational schema *and* an RDF quad store inside the same Postgres instance, then exposes the result through five composable query primitives: typed lookup by IRI, bounded graph neighborhood traversal, read-only SPARQL 1.1, nucleotide substring + reverse-complement search, and ontology-aware role expansion.

See [`docs/crate-guide.md`](https://github.com/marpaia/sbol-db/blob/master/docs/crate-guide.md) for an architectural tour.

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Import SBOL documents and inspect what was stored.

tags.name: Objects
tags.description: Resolve typed SBOL objects by IRI and re-emit them as RDF.

tags.name: SPARQL
tags.description: Read-only SPARQL 1.1 evaluator (SELECT / ASK / CONSTRUCT / DESCRIBE).

tags.name: Sequences
tags.description: Nucleotide substring + reverse-complement search backed by the k-mer seed index.

tags.name: Ontology
tags.description: Load OBO ontologies (SO, SBO, ...) and resolve transitive `is_a` closure.

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Background work queue: enqueue, inspect, and cancel async jobs (e.g. document imports). Workers claim with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, leases auto-renew, retries are exponentially backed off.

tags.name: Meta
tags.description: Liveness, readiness, metrics, OpenAPI schema, and this docs page.

get.description: Static liveness probe. Returns `ok` if the process is running; does not touch Postgres. Wire this to Kubernetes `livenessProbe`.
Issues a short-timeout `SELECT 1` against the connection pool. Returns 200 when Postgres is reachable, 503 otherwise. Wire this to Kubernetes `readinessProbe`.

Prometheus text-format exposition of HTTP request counters/histograms (keyed by templated route), `sbol_db_pool_connections` gauges, and a `sbol_db_build_info` label gauge. Intended for in-cluster scraping; not part of the public query API.

post.description: Parses the request body as an SBOL document in the format derived from the `format` query parameter or the `Content-Type` header, persists it inside one transaction (objects + quads + typed projections + validation), and returns an import report.

requestBody.description: The serialized RDF body for the SBOL document.

post.description: Imports up to 100 documents inside one Postgres transaction. **Either every document commits or none do** — if any document fails to parse or persist, the entire batch is rolled back and the first error is returned unchanged. Per-document validation runs and projection events are still recorded individually, but they share the outer transaction's atomicity.

get.summary: Resolve a stored SBOL object by IRI

get.description: Lists stored objects in lexicographic IRI order with a keyset cursor. Use this for whole-corpus dumps; per-page size capped at 5000. Pass the response's `next_cursor` as `after` on the next call until `next_cursor` is null.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor: last `iri` from the prior page.

200.description: Page of matching objects plus a cursor for the next page (null when exhausted).

post.summary: Bulk resolution of objects by IRI
post.description: Batched form of `GET /objects?iri=…`. Resolves up to 1000 IRIs in a single round trip via `WHERE iri = ANY(...)`. The response separates the IRIs that resolved (`found`) from those that did not (`missing`).

get.summary: Re-emit an object subgraph as RDF

get.description: Walks the graph outward from `iri` under explicit depth, direction, and predicate-allowlist bounds. Returns visited nodes and edges as JSON. See [`docs/neighborhood.md`](https://github.com/marpaia/sbol-db/blob/master/docs/neighborhood.md).

200.description: Visited subgraph in the chosen format.

get.description: Read-only SPARQL endpoint. Update queries are rejected. The result format defaults to `application/sparql-results+json` for SELECT / ASK and `text/turtle` for CONSTRUCT / DESCRIBE; override via `Accept` header or the `format` query parameter.

parameters.name: query
parameters.example: PREFIX sbol: <http://sbols.org/v3#> SELECT ?s WHERE { ?s a sbol:Component } LIMIT 10

get.description: Exact-match search over every indexed nucleotide `sbol:Sequence`. The k-mer seed index covers queries ≥ 8 bp; shorter patterns fall back to an `ILIKE` candidate scan. See [`docs/sequences.md`](https://github.com/marpaia/sbol-db/blob/master/docs/sequences.md).

200.description: Hits sorted by sequence then position.

post.description: Runs the GET form for each pattern in the request body. Capped at 256 patterns per call. The response preserves input order with one `BatchSequenceMatch` entry per pattern.

post.summary: Fetch an OBO ontology and load it
post.description: Recognised prefixes (`so`, `sbo`) resolve to OBO Foundry defaults; for any other prefix supply `url` (and optionally `name`). The terms, aliases, and transitive `is_a` closure are materialised in one transaction. Reload replaces the previous version atomically.

get.summary: Get an ontology term by IRI or CURIE
Page through every term in a loaded ontology
get.description: Returns the terms belonging to `prefix`, sorted by curie, optionally filtered by a case-insensitive substring match against either the curie or the name. The response includes the total match count so clients can render pagination without a second round-trip.

Returns the canonical term plus every descendant under it. Accepts the canonical OBO Foundry IRI (`http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/SO_0000167`), the identifiers.org form, or a bare CURIE (`SO:0000167`).

post.description: Inserts a row in `sbol_jobs`. If `idempotency_key` is set and a non-terminal or succeeded job already exists for the same `(kind, idempotency_key)` pair, the existing job is returned with `deduplicated: true` rather than re-queued.

200.description: The job row, plus a flag indicating whether it was deduplicated against an existing entry.

get.description: Filters compose with AND. The default `limit` is 100; values above 1000 are clamped. Results are ordered most-recent-first.

post.summary: Cancel a queued or running job
post.description: Idempotent: transitions `queued` or `running` jobs to `cancelled` and returns `{ cancelled: true }`. Terminal jobs (succeeded / failed / dead / already cancelled) are left alone and return `{ cancelled: false }`.

200.description: Whether this call transitioned the job.

get.summary: Compact health + traffic snapshot for the lab observability page
get.description: Returns process health, the API + worker pool capacity, job queue depth + oldest-queued age, recent failure counts, and a rolling 10-minute traffic window (request rate, latency quantiles, error counts).

200.description: Total bytes for the current database plus top-10 schemas.

get.summary: Top tables by total relation size

200.description: Per-table stats joined from pg_stat_user_tables + pg_total_relation_size.

get.summary: Top indexes by on-disk size (with idx_scan)

200.description: Index stats with idx_scan = 0 entries flagged for review.
Per-pid live state, excluding the requester's own backend.
Blocking lock pairs (read-only — no terminate action in v1).

get.summary: Schema introspection for a single table
get.description: Columns (with type, nullability, default, comment, primary-key flag) plus outgoing and incoming foreign-key references. Always reads from the `public` schema; sbol-db doesn't expose Postgres schemas as a domain concept. Returns 404 when the relation does not exist.

200.description: Full schema metadata for the requested table.

get.summary: Top slow queries from pg_stat_statements (if installed)
get.description: Returns `{ status: "not_installed", setup_hint }` when the extension isn't present; otherwise `{ status: "installed", rows }`.

get.summary: Recent job history across all queues

NeighborhoodPredicates.name: predicates
NeighborhoodPredicates.description: Comma-separated predicate IRIs (axum's default query parser does not support repeated keys).

NeighborhoodLiterals.name: literals
NeighborhoodLiterals.description: Include literal-position edges in the result (does not affect the traversal frontier).

NotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist.

SparqlResult.description: Serialized SPARQL result. The content type matches the format the engine produced.

body.description: RDF payload in the format named by `format`.

next_cursor.description: IRI to pass as `after` on the next call; absent or null when the listing is exhausted.

prefix.description: Short prefix used as the row key (e.g. `so`, `sbo`).

url.description: Required for prefixes without a built-in default.

name.description: Display name. Defaults to the prefix when omitted.

kind.description: Handler name. Currently only `import_document` is registered.

result.description: Handler-specific JSON result; populated after success.

error.description: Last error message; populated after failure.

available_at.description: Earliest time the job is eligible for dequeue (used by retry backoff).

leased_by.description: Worker id holding the lease while `status = running`.

idempotency_key.description: When set, deduplicates against any non-terminal or succeeded job with the same `(kind, idempotency_key)`.

deduplicated.description: True when an existing job was returned instead of inserting a new one.
